video: Video 3: Create an Asset Library with Megascans (11:10)

node_sequence.description: Import .usdc file with unpack to polygons enabled
node_sequence.notes: Downloads from Fab/Megascans in USDC format (high quality). Unpack to polygons must be ON.

node_sequence.description: Scale from Unreal to Houdini units
node_sequence.notes: Megascans assets are in Unreal units (centimeters). Houdini uses meters. Scale by 0.01.

node_sequence.description: Ground the asset by justifying Y minimum
node_sequence.notes: Ensures the asset sits on the ground plane. Justify Y: Minimum moves the bounding box bottom to Y=0.
Optional — only needed if asset orientation is wrong after import.

node_sequence.description: Generate proxy and sim proxy geometry
node_sequence.notes: 5% reduction. Connect output to both 'proxy' AND 'sim proxy' outputs of Component Geometry.
Ensures correct export from the SOP context.

constraints: Megascans .usdc files contain BOTH geometry and materials in layers — they must be imported separately.
Geometry: USD Import inside Component Geometry SOPs with unpack to polygons ON.
Materials: Use a Reference LOP pointing to the SAME .usdc file with /materials/* wildcard.
Material save location must be 'asset/mtl/' to match the component hierarchy.
Use Paste Relative Reference on file paths so you only need to change one path per asset.
Scale factor 0.01 converts from Unreal centimeters to Houdini meters.
Match Size with Justify Y: Minimum grounds the asset on the ground plane.
PolyReduce output connects to BOTH proxy and sim proxy outputs.
test_criteria: Import a Megascans .usdc file: (1) USD Import with unpack to polygons produces geometry. (2) Transform 0.01 scales correctly. (3) Match Size grounds the asset. (4) PolyReduce 5% produces proxy geo. (5) Reference LOP with /materials/* imports all materials. (6) Material save location 'asset/mtl/' allows Component Material to find them. (7) Component Output exports a valid .usd with both geo and materials.

material_import_trick.description: Megascans .usdc files contain both geometry and materials. To import materials separately: use a Reference LOP pointing to the same .usdc file, set primitive target to /materials/* (wildcard catches all material names), set save location to asset/mtl/ so Component Geometry can find them.
material_import_trick.paste_relative_reference: Use Paste Relative Reference on file paths so only one path change per asset
